  },
  "active_set": ["R1.1", "R1.2", "R1.3", "R1.6", "RX.1", "RX.2", "RX.3", "RX.4", "RX.5"],
  "category_focus": "R1",
  "clarification_needed": true,
  "clarification_question": "By “Clean this up,” which scope do you want: A) language/grammar/conciseness edits only with no technical changes; B) resolve internal technical inconsistencies and align claims with the described implementation/results (e.g., ‘lock-free/wait-free’ vs use of mutexes), revising wording but not changing experiments; or C) both B plus light section-level reorganization and positioning? Please choose A, B, or C.",
  "rationale": {
    "R1.1": "Conflicting statements about progress guarantees versus the described locking suggest the thesis/contribution needs reframing for conceptual coherence.",
    "R1.2": "There are apparent logical inconsistencies (e.g., wait-free/lock-free claims alongside mutex usage) that require verification and correction in the argument chain.",
    "R1.3": "Claims about linearizability, progress, and scaling should be checked against the implementation description and reported results for alignment.",
    "R1.6": "The request to “Clean this up” typically prioritizes sentence-level clarity, grammar, and concision.",
    "RX.1": "Ambiguity in the request and contradictions in the text warrant explicitly flagging assumptions and uncertainties about intended scope.",
    "RX.2": "Strong claims about progress guarantees risk overstatement and should be carefully bounded to avoid overclaiming.",
    "RX.3": "Adhering strictly to the chosen cleanup scope prevents drifting into redesigning algorithms or experiments.",
    "RX.4": "Avoiding misleading technical guidance is important because incorrect concurrency claims can cause downstream system reliability issues.",
    "RX.5": "Any revisions should remain traceable to the original text and clearly indicate sources of assertions and changes."
  }
}

parsed.clarification_question: By “Clean this up,” which scope do you want: A) language/grammar/conciseness edits only with no technical changes; B) resolve internal technical inconsistencies and align claims with the described implementation/results (e.g., ‘lock-free/wait-free’ vs use of mutexes), revising wording but not changing experiments; or C) both B plus light section-level reorganization and positioning? Please choose A, B, or C.

rationale.R1.1: Conflicting statements about progress guarantees versus the described locking suggest the thesis/contribution needs reframing for conceptual coherence.
rationale.R1.2: There are apparent logical inconsistencies (e.g., wait-free/lock-free claims alongside mutex usage) that require verification and correction in the argument chain.
rationale.R1.3: Claims about linearizability, progress, and scaling should be checked against the implementation description and reported results for alignment.
rationale.R1.6: The request to “Clean this up” typically prioritizes sentence-level clarity, grammar, and concision.
rationale.RX.1: Ambiguity in the request and contradictions in the text warrant explicitly flagging assumptions and uncertainties about intended scope.
rationale.RX.2: Strong claims about progress guarantees risk overstatement and should be carefully bounded to avoid overclaiming.
rationale.RX.3: Adhering strictly to the chosen cleanup scope prevents drifting into redesigning algorithms or experiments.
rationale.RX.4: Avoiding misleading technical guidance is important because incorrect concurrency claims can cause downstream system reliability issues.
rationale.RX.5: Any revisions should remain traceable to the original text and clearly indicate sources of assertions and changes.
